ground_truth.title: The 10 Hottest Housing Markets for 2025
ground_truth.author: Susan Kelleher
ground_truth.main_content: In the midst of volatile mortgage rates and affordability challenges for first-time home buyers, local markets could be experiencing a real estate boom. Zillow economists are forecasting for a number of locales in the Northeast and Midwest, where relatively low home prices, job growth and stiff competition have landed a new crop of metros onto Zillow's list of hottest markets for 2025. In some metros, homes are selling in as little as a week, nearly three weeks less than the national average as of November 2024. The fierce demand for homes in all the hottest markets means that prices are expected to rise this year. Zillow predicts that 18 metros will experience faster home value growth in 2025 than they did a year earlier. To come up with the list of hottest markets, Zillow analyzed the 50 largest U.S. metro areas for home value growth, projected change in owner-occupied households, job growth compared to new construction, and the speed at which homes are sold. Only four metros from last year's list made the cut for 2025, representing a shift away from some of the hot markets in Florida and Ohio. The top 10 hottest large metros for 2025 are: 1. Buffalo, NY - tops the list for the second year in a row, with typical home value forecast to rise 2.8% to $267,878, homes sell in 12 days. 2. Indianapolis, IN - typical home value expected to rise to $285,086, new listings go pending in about two weeks. 3. Providence, RI - home values expected to grow by about 3.7%, homes go pending in 12 days. 4. Hartford, CT - homes forecast to reach $378,693 in 2025, a 4.2% increase, homes sell in about 7 days. 5. Philadelphia, PA - modest 2.6% growth projected, new listings go pending in an average of 11 days. 6. St. Louis, MO - rated Zillow's top market for first-time buyers in 2024, typical home value to reach $254,847, homes sell in about eight days. 7. Charlotte, NC - typical home value expected to appreciate about 3.2% to $389,383, homes go pending in about 20 days. 8. Kansas City, MO - typical home worth $307,334, homes go from listing to pending in 9 days. 9. Richmond, VA - forecasted growth in home values of 2.9%, homes sell in an average of 9 days. 10. Salt Lake City, UT - typical home value to reach $555,858, homes sell in about 19 days. To succeed in these competitive markets, buyers should prepare by getting finances in order and getting pre-approved with a lender so they can move quickly. An experienced real estate agent can help with local market conditions and competitive strategies for making an offer.
with: In the midst of volatile mortgage rates and affordability challenges for first-time home buyers, local markets could be experiencing a real estate boom.

Zillow economists are forecasting for a number of locales in the Northeast and Midwest, where relatively low home prices, job growth and stiff competition have landed a new crop of metros onto Zillow's list of hottest markets for 2025.

To come up with the list of hottest markets, Zillow analyzed the 50 largest U.S. metro areas for home value growth, projected change in owner-occupied households, job growth compared to new construction, and the speed at which homes are sold.
Buffalo tops our hottest markets list for the second year in a row, with typical home value forecast to rise 2.8% to $267,878, with homes going from listing to pending in only 12 days.

Prepare yourself by getting your finances in order, and by getting pre-approved with a lender so that you can move quickly, especially in markets like Kansas City, where homes go from listing to pending in as little as nine days.
